summary:
- **Core topic/purpose:** Colonial Office and Malayan administration correspondence and legislation on **wartime taxation**—especially the introduction/administration of a **War Tax (income-tax type) ordinance** and **additional export duties** as war-finance measures.  
- **Geography & period:** Focuses on the **Straits Settlements (Singapore, Penang, Malacca)** and the **Federated Malay States (F.M.S.)**, mainly **1941** (with measures applying into **1942**).  
- **Major proposals/decisions/events:**  
  - Debate over using **export duties (rubber/tin)** as a “rough and ready” alternative/adjunct to income tax; some officials object that export duty is a poor substitute and could harm **UK companies** (Jan 1941 minutes).  
  - Decision to proceed with/approve **increased rubber and tin export duties** (not all details legible, but referenced as approved by telegram).  
  - **Income tax introduced/renewed as war taxation** in the Straits Settlements (first time since shortly after WWI, per internal note), with ongoing amendments and later **rate increases**.  
- **Key actors:** **Governor Sir Shenton (S.) Thomas** (Straits Settlements); **Colonial Office** (incl. Lord **Moyne**); officials/minute-writers including **W.B.L. Monson**, **G.E.J. Gent**, **Sir G. Burns**, **Mr. Caine**, **Roberts-Wray**; **Board of Inland Revenue (UK)** and **Treasury** consulted on double-taxation/administration and transfers.  
- **Notable legislation & dates (Straits Settlements):**  
  - **War Tax (Additional Duties) Ordinance, 1941 (No. 6 of 1941)** assented **30 Apr 1941** (increased stamp duties; additional duties on bets/sweepstakes).  
  - **War Tax (Amendment) Ordinance, 1941 (No. 35 of 1941)** assented **27 Aug 1941** (broadened definition of “company,” incl. foreign companies with business presence).  
  - **War Tax (Amendment No. 2) Ordinance, 1941 (No. 48 of 1941)** assented **16 Oct 1941** (multiple amendments incl. exemptions, trustee withholding at **8%**, etc.).  
  - **War Tax (Additional Rubber Duty) (No. 2) Ordinance, 1941 (No. 67 of 1941)** assented **19 Dec 1941**, effective **22 Dec 1941**: **additional 5% of value** export duty on rubber exported during **1942**, proceeds for war expenses only; report notes it **continues** and **increases** earlier additional duty (mentions a further **2½%** increase in context).  
- **Notable taxation parameters (War Tax Bill for 1942, per Gov. telegram 12 Nov 1941):** scale from **2%** (incomes ≥ **$5,000**) up to **12%** (incomes ≥ **$20,000**); **surtax** bands up to **20%** on remainder above specified thresholds; **marriage allowance $2,000** to “individual residents”; aggregation of husband/wife incomes; **flat 12%** for incorporated companies and for non-resident individuals on income arising/received in the Colony; lower limit later noted as reduced **$4,800 → $3,000** and top rate **8% → 12%** (internal summary).  
- **War-finance transfers (notable numbers):** Requests/approvals to transfer proceeds of war taxation locally to military paymasters: **$1,130,000** (Apr–Jun 1941 quarter) and **$1,852,726.51** (quarter ended Sep 1941); Treasury concurred in conveying H.M.G.’s thanks/approval for transfers.
